commands.review_summary:
The PR changes the Chrome extension SSE parser to preserve `data` whitespace and adds parser tests for whitespace and colonless data fields.

Reproducibility: yes. from source inspection: current main trims each `data:` field and trims the accumulated payload at dispatch, so leading and trailing data whitespace is lost. I did not run the parser because this review is read-only.
commands.review_followup: - [P1] A maintainer should wait for contributor-supplied real behavior proof or decide whether green CI is enough for this draft PR.

target.title: fix: preserve SSE data whitespace in extension parser
target.body:
## Summary

- Parse extension SSE fields by splitting on the first colon so colonless `data` lines are treated as empty data fields.
- Preserve significant leading and trailing whitespace in `data` values while still removing the single optional space after the colon.
- Remove only the parser-appended final newline when dispatching multiline SSE data.

## Why

The Chrome extension SSE parser previously used `trim()`/`trimEnd()` on `data:` payloads. That silently changed valid SSE payload text with meaningful leading or trailing whitespace and skipped the colonless `data` field case defined by SSE parsing rules.

## Validation

- `pnpm -s build`
- `pnpm -s check`
target.author: vincent-peng
